classObjective: Identify rigid motions (reflection, translation, rotation) from diagrams.
workedExamples: 11-4, Example 1 — Identify Rigid Motions from Diagrams
warmUp: What are the three types of rigid motions?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: reflection (flip), translation (slide), rotation (turn); identifying preimage-image pairs; rigid motion properties
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice identifying rigid motions from preimage-image diagram pairs
independentPractice: Practice problems identifying which rigid motion maps one figure to another
assessment: Exit ticket: identify whether each transformation shown is a reflection, translation, or rotation
capReflection: What properties do rigid motions preserve that non-rigid motions do not?
